reviews.review_text: One of the most beloved games, “Mad Max” is a fantastic open-world game that excels in both its storyline and graphics, as well as its post-apocalyptic atmosphere. And the process of upgrading your vehicle is a real joy as you travel through the desert.

Nostalgic game, with more fun and detail than today's so-called AAA slop.
Not the greatest story, but it certainly has a vast and engaging world.
Hope it gets remastered in the future +_+

I [b]DO NOT[/b] recommend collecting everything and getting 100% achievements. But if you want to: save often and use guides. There are [b]BUGS[/b] [spoiler]not only once Max loves to crunch on[/spoiler].
A decent game with nice car controls, cinematic moments [spoiler]especially while driving during storm[/spoiler]. An awsome world, that almost does not feel empty with all the action you can wish for in a Mad Max world.

An old game now but a really good unique game. Nothing like it.
If your a fan of the mad max world and loved fury road, this is really for you and it really builds on the world (minus the rampant guns from fury road).
It really explores the world and how people lived in it, the car racing around the wasteland, different vibrant locations and well the horror of the apocalyptic world. The engines and making your custom car is awsome.
The negatives It doesn't go far enough with the customisation of the cars in my opinion and some of the later games locked parts seem really annoying, why do i need to clear an entire region for the best engine in the game. It can get a bit repetitive near the end and it feel like it could have gone deeper in the story and world a bit. There are different factions who seemingly hate each other but combat between them is rarely seen. And the other factions aern't as explored. Well the road kill faction. Who i feel like were just invented in the game as a filler faction but with little point. 
Overall though a good game and i hope they make another one. I keep coming back to this game every so often... thats how good it is.... though i hated fighting those buzzards.

Another case of "I played this on console, had to see how it handled on Steam" entry from me. Maybe I have a thing for underappreciated games to boot, but Mad Max really gives a good approximation of being Max out in the wasteland. Total guzzline, ca-chunk ca-chunk ca-chunk vibes throughout. The driving and vehicular combat are on point, melee/hand to hand feels brutal and has the perfect amount of weighty crunch to it, and the unexpected cosmetic aspects are actually pretty cool. Watch out for the storms, and trust Chumbucket, he won't steer ya wrong. Keep an eye out for aqua-cola, you will need it, and begin to resent it's absence.

war rig when also when will miller direct mad max game?
also jusice for scrap upgrates stop killing games!!!
A underrated gem of a game, sad it doesnt get more recognition. Combat mechanics good, driving mechanics. Personally in my opinion of liking post apocalyptic games, its a good one.
Fantastic game. It has a slow burn in the beginning but this game is worth investing into. Especially once you start upgrading him and go beat the camps.

WHAT A GAME!! To think that I bought this game for just $4.49 AUD to me is just criminal. The amount of content packed in with such an awesome story for that value is crazy.

Hats off to the developers at Avalanche studios, the different mechanics in this game are really fun from progressing the story as well as clearing areas controlled by a different stronghold/faction all while upgrading Max and your car (The Magnum Opus) is very fulfilling it doesn't feel that "grindy" as for me I can't say that any of the areas or camps are cookie cutter remakes where it becomes boring or "going through the motions" for example with games like Mafia 3 or Assassin's Creed where it is unlock this synch point do this do that rinse and repeat, whereas this game each level feels unique, don't get me wrong there are some points that are similar but with the added upgrade features you get to test out your upgraded gear and skills in hand to hand combat as well as vehicular combat which keeps things refreshing.

The game design is really cool I love the wasteland feel as any fan of the movies would as well as the added Australian accent, the sound design is also really cool where you can really hear the sound of the V6 or V8 engine as well as various explosions and the story is very good I shed a tear at the end which I have only ever done before with games like Ghost of Tsushima or The Last of Us.

Phenomenal game 10/10 Truly an underrated gem
Such an underrated game way ahead of its time! Even in 2026 it still holds up to being amazing!

Fun game but avoid using a controller

Refusing to keep weapons is bad.

Bought this game years ago on Xbox, but when it was the same price as a cup of coffee on steam sale, I had to get it again! I love this game, definitely shows its age in 2026, but the wasteland is great and this game deserves a sequel. Even a Furiosa game would be cool. 

Its a shame you cant obtain all the achievements since one of the requirements in the last achievement requires a connection to a server which isn't up anymore
Good game, cars are a little slippery, but ♥♥♥♥ YOU WARNER BROS FOR MAKING 100% IMPOSSIBLE.
A very fun game; this is one of the better post-apocalyptic franchises

what a blast. very fun combat and driving is very fun. I am not very good at the racing part of the game, esp with the Gas Town race; So I did not enjoy the mechanics there. Side quests gets pretty repetitive pretty quickly so if you want to 100%, it can be a drag. Very little to no bugs encountered. graphics still holds up in 2026. definitely recommend.

Love this game soo much, story is peak, and the sounds of those screaming v6 and v8 engines is so amazing, fighting is good, after story it becomes a bit boring to complete all death races and collectibles but this game definitely has a special place in my hearth
I love this game.Yes it was made in 2015 but man, everything the game tries to do it does it well. I have played and beat this game twice on Xbox ages ago. Happy to buy and play it again lmao

Although its mission design is somewhat repetitive, Mad Max offers a highly satisfying open-world action experience thanks to its brutal vehicle combat and stunning post-apocalyptic desert setting. Also, I finished this game exactly 8 hours before my grandmother passed away. R.I.P Grandma.

I really liked it. The gameplay is fun, and the driving even more so. Exploring the world and upgrading your gear and your car to the max is satisfying. It’s a game that perfectly fits the definition of “a 7/10”: it doesn’t deserve more than a 7, but within that “7,” it’s the best it can be.

That said, after a while, the combat and driving become repetitive. The story is weak, and getting some upgrades takes a long time, especially because of the mine-disarming missions. Still, it’s worth playing and completing. Buy it on sale ;).

I got Mad Max for free on PS Plus nearly a decade ago, and I don’t remember why, but I very quickly fell off of the game. Fast forward to finally giving it a shot on PC, and most of the time, it was a blast! The graphics are surprisingly beautiful, driving progressively improved and felt weighty, the sound design for the cars are incredible, the combat is a slightly harder yet a less varied version of the Arkham games, the upgrade progression is enjoyable (to a certain point), and the story made some choices by the end that I was surprised by. There are some obvious highs and lows to this game, to the point where I wish there was a “mixed” option rather than a “thumbs-up” or “thumbs-down”.

I recommend this game, but I absolutely do not recommend trying to 100% complete it or even come close to that. As you can see, I only played this game for about 20 hours, and due to a lot of compounding issues, I was growing tired of it. It all comes back to the game’s upgrade progression. In order to upgrade Max and his car, you have to collect massive amounts of scrap which are mostly found in bandit camps. The problem is that these camps feel so samey and repetitive that I just wanted to finish the story and be done with the game after many hours of grinding. 

The camps have basically the same objectives every time, there are four enemy types even though you deal with them all in the same way, all of the bosses or horrendously health-spongy and are all the same, and searching for scrap becomes such a chore that I may have gotten all the scrap in three camps total. I also quickly fell off from going after scarecrows unless they were on my path since it sucks to exit the car to pick up the scrap you get for destroying them. 

All of this compounded into me having a relatively weak build for the final few missions, making them doable, but needlessly frustrating with no way to change the difficulty other than by grinding. This was despite the fact that I completed at least 90% of the camps in the game. There are even parts of the story that require you collect scrap for upgrades or you can’t progress. 

There is a common law in open world games where the player should be able to choose how much they want to explore and do side content before finishing the story. Easy examples are The Witcher 3, Zelda BoTW, RDR2, Cyberpunk 2077, Horizon, and Fallout. Many of these games allow you to change their difficulty to lessen any tedium. Funnily enough, I never had to do that with any of the games since I greatly enjoyed their worlds and gameplay, sometimes for over a hundred hours, but I wish Mad Max had this option since it clearly doesn’t have the depth to keep me going for even a fraction of these other games’ run-time. 

I think this game was too ambitious for its own good as they forced the player to have to do a ton of blatantly similar side content to comfortably progress in the story (even though I was not comfortable). If they just made the world 30% smaller and required less scrap for quality of life upgrades, like the car weaponry, Mad Max would be far better paced. I still really enjoyed driving around in the world and atmosphere of Mad Max, seeing and hearing the changes to my car, blowing stuff up, and witnessing Max’s struggle to be human, but you can only do so much of that before it gets very old.

The game is beautiful and scenic. Art designers created probably the most palpable and convincing post apocalyptic world, the environment creates this strange longing to know about the world which is now buried under the sand, the world is nihilistic, every living being exercises their will for power.
my favorite character was scrotus's brother peenar, and their best friend pretty lips.
good game, fun and you can enjoy it but dont expect too much for the ending of the  game. ( everyone dies)
Played this game and ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ milked it for everything it had, had that gamey feeling games lack these days. Loved it.

Arkham meets Far Cry in the wasteland. And somehow both better and worse than that sounds.

I wanted something different, and in that sense, Mad Max absolutely delivered. It has that dry, dirty, rusty atmosphere that fits the license very well, and for a 2015 movie tie-in game, it is honestly better than expected. The graphics still hold up surprisingly well for a 10-year-old game, the wasteland has a strong identity, and when the game is focused on cars, explosions, scrap, and chaos, it works.

The car is easily the best part of the game. Driving around, upgrading the Magnum Opus, ramming enemies, chasing convoys, and watching cars explode is where Mad Max feels most alive. Convoys especially can actually be challenging, which makes them stand out in a game where most of the on-foot combat barely pushes back. The upgrade loop also gives scavenging a purpose, at least early on. Scrap matters, car parts matter, and for a while it feels like you are slowly building your way through the wasteland.

But the more I played, the more the game started working against itself.

At first, I was willing to be positive about the dated feel. The controls are clunky, the world is broken, everything feels old and rough, and weirdly, that kind of fits Mad Max. But after several hours, that charm starts to fade. The controls feel outdated, especially the shooting, with the strange RB/LB aiming and firing setup. Shooting from the car feels even worse than shooting on foot, which is a shame because the vehicle gameplay is otherwise the strongest part of the game.

The on-foot combat is clearly inspired by the Arkham games, but it never comes close to them. Enemies surround you, mostly wait their turn, and you hit, dodge, and parry your way through fights. It is functional, sometimes satisfying, but very limited. Even when tougher enemies start showing up, they do not add much challenge on normal difficulty. The hardest enemy is not a boss or a new enemy type, it is the camera in tight spaces, you often cannot properly see where enemies are, which makes parrying harder for the wrong reason.

The open-world structure reminds a lot of Far Cry. Reveal parts of the map through balloons, reduce enemy presence, raid camps, take over strongholds, collect scrap, upgrade things, repeat. None of this is terrible by itself. In fact, some of it is enjoyable in small doses. The problem is proportion. The amount of repetitive content is massive compared to the amount of meaningful story.

The animations also get worse over time. Entering the car, breaking doors, filling the water bottle, drinking water, healing.. they all help sell the world. But after 10, 12, 14 hours, they become animation tax. Every little delay adds up. What starts as immersion slowly turns into friction.

Mad Max is not bad. That is important. It is just stretched way beyond what its mechanics can support. There is a solid, fun, dirty wasteland action game here, but it is buried under too much grind, too many icons, too many repeated camps, and too much friction.

TL;DR: Mad Max is a surprisingly decent game and a fun wasteland car combat game trapped inside a bloated open-world. The car gameplay is genuinely enjoyable, but the Arkham-style combat is shallow, the grind is disproportionate, and the game slowly becomes more tiring than exciting. 7/10.
i would highly recommend this game, it never seems to get old. The atmosphere, the car combat, the ground combat, this game is great.

a beautiful game that requires no knowledge on how to play and by the end you are kicking ass. it is a little buggy but nothing that will break the game which is good for a game with no development for 10 years. the graphics look amazing comparable if not better than most AAA games today, and this game is from 2015 so i can only imagine the boundries it broke back then
The fact that this game was forgotten by WB is a terrible sin. If you like Batman's freeflow combat system within the Mad Max setting it's well worth a play.
very fun game especially the fight scenes the vehicle combat and the open world to explore
Cool game but the wb gaming servers closed in 2020 so you cant have access to online play but still the best game ever made 10/10

gameplay - 10/10
world - 10/10

buy or not? - the best time to buy the game was yesterday.

Be the Saint and drive your Opus Magnus beast through vast dystopian immersive deserts, Chumbucket spewing saintly mechanical verses in the back, fight crazies in cars and on foot, harpooning, exploding, ramming and executing them as you please, live the gnarly tale of a mentally broken but refusing to give up or die lucid madman, enjoy the dramatic skies and electric storms and aura farm like never before in a dying world of dust and derangedness. It's bleak, it's deep, it's dark and it's powerful.

epic game...wish theyd do something about online even if it was just open world destruction i mean jeez how lazy do people get once theyve sold these games......come on even the mod guys could make some open world mad max carnage....would be cool...overall rad game...too bad maxs destiny is always one of solitude.
Great game good combat both in and out of the car great soundtrack and story
very well optimized game lol looks amazing but runs at max settings with a 2009 pc setup lmao
Underrated yet fantastic, with a lot of content and many things to blow up. Recommended to both fans of Mad Max movies and people who are just looking for a great open world game.

This is kind of a hidden gem. It flew under the radar for a lot of people and is a solid game. It's not groundbreaking, but it's a lot of fun. Simply putting you behind the wheel of a Mad Max car makes a lot of difference in how fun it is to traverse an open world.

The sound and music are also distinctly great.

The game has aged well, and even in 2025 was still a lot of fun!
The world is well recreated, the combat is satisfying, and driving crazy armored cars is pure joy.
The main story is quite long (which is a good thing), and clearing the map will take quite some time.

I was nice surprised with two big quests after you defeated final Big Boss.
Its was not firsttime i played this game but i was enjoy it realy alot play it again as enhanced remake.
I wish you safe ride...

I'm surprised this game isn't talked as much as some "batman arkham clones" of it's time.
Ok, I most definetely am a sucker for the story of Mad Max (my original novel is largely based on it!), but even so, this game is just so much fun, I could be trapped playing it for days on end (and I did!), the whole upgrading, battle, car race + tricks, and overall mechanics makes this an experience you can lose yourself very easy (compliment), and even tho I could see it coming, Mad Max being a tragedy and such, the end absolutely broke me, YOU BET YOUR SWEET ASS SCROTUS IS BURNING IN *THERE* RIGHT NOW.
pick this as soon as a sale comes up, worth every cent.
Really fun open world to play if you like post-apocalyptic universes

Something about this game won't let me go. As an open world sandbox, there isn't much of innovation here on a gameplay level, but what it does, it does well. It thought I would get bored of combing the map for the same camps and scrap, but I didn't I think that is because this game nails the world of Mad Max: driving in big, bleak wastelands. Though it's set in the Mad Max 2 world (which includes everything up to Thunderdome chronologically), it hits that sense of sad desolation that the first film had. 

Upgrading the Magnum Opus is almost as satisfying as driving around wiping out Warboys and Buzzards. The on-foot combat is decent (lifted wholesale from Batman, but who cares). But again, all this is almost secondary to the Huge Vibes this game bleeds out of every pixel.

I've seen some people complain about the story, and the passive role Max takes in things. But this is absolutely on-point for the character. The voice acting is great for Max and especially Chumbucket. In many ways, he is the heart of the story. I do wish they hadn't used generic American actors for most of the support roles, however.

The sum of its parts adds up to what Mad Max is on a tonal and thematic level. The driving is slick and thrilling. The world is sad and beautiful.
Underrated gem. Should play in 2026. Great game for fun. One this is that it gets repetitive after some time but still worth a try.
fun game , a bit time consuming , but its cure my doom scrolling

After playing Mad Max, I can say it is one of the most underrated open-world games I’ve played. The wasteland atmosphere is amazing, with deserts, destroyed areas, and brutal survival themes making the world feel immersive and unique. The car combat is easily one of the best parts of the game, especially upgrading the Magnum Opus and fighting enemy vehicles during high-speed chases. Hand-to-hand combat feels similar to the Batman Arkham games and stays satisfying throughout the adventure. The story is simple but fits the harsh world perfectly, while exploration and upgrading give a strong sense of progression. Some missions can become repetitive after many hours, but overall the gameplay, atmosphere, and vehicle combat make Mad Max a very fun and memorable experience.

First of all, I just want to say that this game is incredibly underrated and deserves recognition. It's a truly excellent and accurate depiction of the Mad Max universe. In terms of characters and story, it fits perfectly with the apocalyptic world. The gameplay is incredibly engaging and fun, both in combat and car gameplay. I highly recommend Mad Max. "What a day! What a lovely day!"

This is open-world gaming done to perfection, warts and all.

Good story, good characters, great sound-design, great gameplay, very repetitive; still, the "white dude building his vehicle in a post-apocalyptic setting" is the best version of open-world gaming, if you ask me. 

Mad Max and Days Gone are forever goated classics of gaming.

SCORE: 7/10.

I wish they would make MM2 and release already its the only game ive seen with this type of mechanics.
If its your first time playing just now theres some bug but the game is awesome plz buy
The atmosphere is amazing but level design on certain missions is the worst I've ever seen.
Absolutely peak in 2015, and still peak as of 2026. (pls remaster this game, it would sell so many copies)

Very very underrated game, its a shame it got sent to the dark parts on the shelf, this is an amazing game. The driving is brutal and very cinematic, the fights hold a bit of weight. The story is there, its not bad, but not the greatest in the world. Customization on vehicles are amazing and its soo fun to see all of the stats show on the vehicles as well as on Max himself. Can be a bit repetetive, lots of driving and blowing things up, but very fun to play, love this game.

For a game that revolves around driving, it has the worse driving! However, gameplay is ok, world-building is great, fighting mechanic is fun, but the story couldve been longer and better. Overall, its ok but couldve been better,

nothing changes in the gameplay between the massive zones (some may as well be empty), it gets repetitive and actions for example collecting scrap gets irritating, getting in and out of your car constantly if you don't want things to go to waste or you want the best upgrades. If you're a fan of the film and its on sale I would recommend it, kinda wish there was a middle review option because the game really feels like a time passer most of the time. The graphics can really stand out but the audio of the game is a massive limiting factor - so much of it sounds like it was almost recorded through an iphone, hurting the immersion and adding to the feeling of a husk of a game. should this really have been such a wide open world game?

it should also be noted there are features you come across which cant be used anymore because there's no online connection

[h1] [b] Mad Max Is Absolutely Hidden Gem [/b] [/h1]

The atmosphere is so matching with the vibe of game, the open world is perfect for a Mad Max game, the story was definitely lackluster. However, it's hell of fun driving around, ralling & smashing enemy cars with your beast Magnum Opus.

[i] [b] Story 5/10
Gameplay 8/10 [/b] [/i]

[b] There aren't many road rage + open world games I have to give Avalanche Studios for that, they made a gem and buried this franchise due to lower sales but I still hope to see Mad Max 2 in future <3 [/b]

[h1] [b] Highly Recommended 8/10 [/b] [/h1]

With the upcoming Black Flag remaster, I've been seeing a number of early reviews mentioning "games of that era." That got me thinking about other games I've played and enjoyed from that time and Mad Max is very much one of those games. Ubisoft towers, endless upgrades for your boat...err car, an icon filled map. Of course, we still get all of that stuff in today's games too, but back then they felt a lot more fun and a lot less cynical like they do today. Games of this period had a lot more charm and character to them and this one still holds up. If you're looking for something to play while waiting for the Black Flag remake this is a good choice. The driving is fun and "crunchy" (I f-ing hate that word being used like that) as is the on foot combat which is like a mix of the Batman games and an AC game.

But if there's one thing I want you to take away from this review - don't miss out on this game if for the plot alone. Try to see it through to the end even if you need to skip the icons and come back to them. One of the darkest endings I've ever experienced in a video game.
An amazing combat, open world MASTERPIECE GAME. Always feels like first time playing everytime I replay this masterpiece.
I love this game, being a mad max fan and there a nice mods for the game.

[h1] THE BRUTAL AND BEAUTIFUL WASTELAND [/h1]

[b][i]  67 hours spent surviving the harsh, unforgiving Wasteland. Mad Max is one of the most atmospheric and criminally underrated open-world games out there. The vehicular combat is an absolute masterpiece—upgrading the Magnum Opus and tearing through enemy convoys feels incredibly satisfying.

The hand-to-hand combat is heavy, brutal, and carries a real weight, while the post-apocalyptic desert vistas are breathtakingly beautiful despite their emptiness. If you love high-octane car combat and a gritty survival atmosphere, this journey is essential.

My world is fire and blood. 8.5/10  [/i][/b]
